Pathway_Genes: Petunia hybrida chi-a (chalcone isomerase): Overexpression of the Petunia chi-a gene (encoding chalcone isomerase, CHI) under the CaMV double 35S promoter in tomato (var. FM6203) resulted in up to 78-fold increase in fruit peel flavonols. Hydrolyzed peel extracts showed up to 66-fold increase in quercetin (up to 16.52 mg/g dry weight) and 57-fold increase in kaempferol (up to 2.05 mg/g dry weight) compared to controls. Naringenin chalcone levels were severely depleted in high-flavonol transformants, consistent with increased CHI enzyme activity converting naringenin chalcone to naringenin. Northern analysis confirmed chi transgene transcript was present at low levels in peel at all developmental stages in transgenics, whereas endogenous chi transcript was undetectable in control fruit peel. The high-flavonol phenotype segregated with the chi transgene and was stably inherited over four generations. Nonhydrolyzed extracts showed accumulation of rutin (quercetin 3-rutinoside) and isoquercitrin (quercetin 3-glucoside) in transgenic peel. Processed paste from transgenic fruit retained 65% of flavonols, with up to 1.9 mg flavonols/g dry weight paste (21-fold increase over control). No changes in lycopene concentration or vegetative phenotype were observed.
Title: Overexpression of petunia chalcone isomerase into tomato results in fruit containing increased levels of flavonols
